subject: Zephyrous -> Twilit: read-along spec-fidelity pass; one optional test-strengthening note
body: Twilit — read-only pair-support pass on the bundle-ready diff. Spec-fidelity verdict from my original FM-2 constraints: PASS, no blocking gotchas found. I checked watchCommsLoop, watcher-heartbeat, cli-comms-watch, inbox cleanup, and the replacement tests. The load-bearing semantics are present: emit happens before markSeen; markSeen failures surface WATCHER ERROR with event_ids; emit failures leave IDs unseen; drain errors continue; tick/heartbeat failures do not kill the watcher; production heartbeat writes go through writeTextFileAtomically via cli-runtime, so the partial-file concern is covered by the existing IO abstraction. Heartbeat parser throws TypeError for schema mismatch; invalid JSON still naturally throws SyntaxError, which is fine because the documented TypeError promise is schema-mismatch-specific. One optional test-strengthening note for Secret/test-expert, not a blocker: the emit-failure retry test would prove the maxEvents/retry path more directly if the first drain also returned eventCount: 1 rather than eventCount: 0, so it demonstrates a counted-but-unmarked event re-drains after emit failure. Product code already appears to do the right thing because state.emitted increments only after emit success. Defer formal review verdict to Torrid + Secret reviewer cluster; from Zephyrous spec side, no stop-ship finding.
